core.label: Agda Fundamentals
core.description: Dependently typed language for proofs and verified programs.

topics.label: Installation & Emacs
topics.description: Install Agda with cabal and use the Emacs mode. Agda is primarily used for formal verification.

topics.label: Types & Values
topics.description: Natural numbers, booleans, and user-defined data types. Everything has a type, including types.

topics.label: Pattern Matching
topics.description: Define functions by pattern matching on constructors. Agda checks totality.

topics.label: Dependent Types
topics.description: Types that depend on values. Vec A n — a vector of exactly n elements of type A.

topics.label: Propositions as Types
topics.description: Logical propositions are types, proofs are programs. The Curry-Howard correspondence.

topics.label: Interactive Proof Development
topics.description: Use holes (?) and refine. Agda's emacs mode guides proof construction.

topics.label: Records & Modules
topics.description: Record types for product types, modules for namespacing and parameterization.

topics.label: Standard Library
topics.description: Data.Nat, Data.List, Data.Vec, Relation.Binary. The agda-stdlib.
